agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

Claude Code has a first-class Task tool (a.k.a. Agent tool) that spawns sub-agents for parallel exploration. Each subagent writes an `isSidechain` transcript to `~/.claude/projects/<slug>/<parent>/subagents/agent-<uuid>.jsonl`. The parent waits for the subagent's completion signal — a `task-notification` user-event in the parent transcript carrying the subagent's task-id and status — before continuing its turn.

irrlicht's claudecode adapter observes the full topology via file-based discovery, not in-process counting:

1. The fswatcher walks `~/.claude/projects/` (transcriptsDir(), agent.go:36) and picks up every subagent transcript as its own `SessionState`.
2. The detector emits a `KindParentLinked` lifecycle event when the child session's `ParentSessionID` is set (session_detector_activity.go:110). The orchestrator-side scenario `subagent-spawn` already verifies this with `parent_linked_min: 2`.
3. `CountOpenSubagents` (parser.go:553) deliberately returns 0 because the file-based path is the single source of truth — counting in-process Agent tool_use entries as well would double-count. The metrics layer derives `SubagentCount(parent)` from the live children that link to it.
4. Parent state: the parent's outstanding Agent tool_use prevents `IsAgentDone()` from firing, so the parent stays `working` until the tool_result for the Task arrives. `handleTaskNotification` (parser.go:192) records the subagent completion signals from the parent transcript.

Mapped to spec bullets:
- 'Parent ready → working at spawn' — parent's Agent tool_use line drives the transition.
- 'Child session appears with ParentSessionID = parent.session_id' — fswatcher discovers the sidechain file and links via lifecycle KindParentLinked.
- 'SubagentCount(parent) = N while any child alive' — derived from live linked children.
- 'Parent stays working until child ends' — parent's pending Agent tool_use blocks IsAgentDone.
- 'Parent → ready after last child ends' — task-notification arrives, parent emits end_turn, working→ready.

The AskUserQuestion-from-child variant works because the child has its own independent transcript and tailer state, so its `working→waiting→working→ready` arc is computed exactly like a parent's. The orphan-on-death variant is covered by the sweep — if the child process dies without an end-of-turn line, it's reaped by the same mechanism used in `subagent-orphan-cleanup` (assessed separately).
caveats: claudecode's subagent transcripts live under `<parent>/subagents/agent-*.jsonl` — a path that the curator's fixture bundler treats specially (`<fixture>.subagents/`). The orchestrator-side `subagent-spawn` recipe already validates the end-to-end shape; the agent-level coverage_id maps to `foreground-subagent` directly.

The N=3 parallel variant works because the file-based path scales linearly — each sidechain transcript is an independent SessionState. No special-case parser logic is needed for the 1-vs-3 distinction.

sources.note: CountOpenSubagents (line 553) returns 0 by design — file-based sidechain transcripts are the single source of truth, avoiding double-counting against open Agent tool_use entries.
lifecycle.KindParentLinked emitted at line 110 when a child SessionState's ParentSessionID is bound — this is what the orchestrator-side verify asserts.
subagent-spawn recipe (coverage_id: foreground-subagent) — prompts 3 parallel Explore subagents and asserts parent_linked_min: 2, subagent_transcripts_min: 2.

preconditions: claude CLI on PATH and authenticated (subscription or API key)
irrlichd is recording (--attach against a daemon with --record, or isolated mode)
claude session can spawn at least 1 subagent (default model has Task/Agent tool exposed)
setup: Driver generates a fresh lowercased session UUID and a fresh cwd under <staging>/cwd/ (claude's trust dialog appears once and the driver auto-accepts)
A README.md with a single H1 heading is seeded into the cwd so the subagent has a deterministic file to read

script.text: Use the Task tool to launch a general-purpose subagent that reads the README.md in the current directory and reports the first heading. Wait for the subagent to finish, then reply 'done'.

verify: events.jsonl shows the parent UUID entering ready, then a single ready→working transition driven by the user prompt
events.jsonl contains at least 1 parent_linked event pointing at the parent UUID — the child sidechain transcript was discovered and bound
transcript.jsonl contains a tool_use block with name=Agent (the Task tool invocation)
the subagent transcript is present under ~/.claude/projects/<slug>/<parent>/subagents/agent-<uuid>.jsonl
no state_transition to ready or waiting fires on the parent UUID between parent_linked and the final working→ready (parent stays working until the child reports back)
events.jsonl shows exactly one working→ready transition for the parent UUID at turn end
final session state for the parent is ready
